rulings.Case Title: MASON LIVINGSTON ET AL VS. 23ANDME, INC. A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Complaint And Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 14, 2015, Line 11, PETITIONER MASON LIVINGSTON, VLADMIR DRUSKIN Hearing On Petitioners Complaint And Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award. Denied. "In order to challenge an award in court, a litigant must have raised the point before the arbitrator." (Comerica Bank v. Howsam (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 790, 829.) Otherwise, the issue is waived. (Zazueta v. Cnty. of San Benito (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 106, 111.) Here, Petitioners argue that the arbitrator erred by basing his decision on cases applying the FAA, and not California law. Petitioners did not raise this argument at the arbitration. In fact, Petitioners argued in their arbitration brief that "[w]ithin the broad, federal boundaries established by Bazzle, Stolt-Nielsen, and Oxford Health, the Arbitrator should be guided by state principles of contract law in determining whether the Arbitration Clause allows for class arbitration." (Talarides Decl., Ex. D, Arbitration brief, p. 5.) This is precisely what the Arbitrator did in rendering his decision. (See Talarides Decl., Ex. A, Arbitration Award, pp. 4-8.) Further, Petitioners take issue with the fact that the agreement in the two primary cases relied on by the Arbitrator, Kinecta Alternative Financial Solutions, Inc. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 506 and Nelsen v. Legacy Partners Residential, Inc. (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 1115, expressly invoked the FAA. Petitioners' reply arbitration brief makes clear that this argument was not advanced in the arbitration. Rather, Petitioners argued at the arbitration that Kinecta and Nelsen are distinguishable because the arbitration clause is broader in this case and Oxford Health Plans LLC v. Sutter (2013) 133 S.Ct. 2064 limited the Stolt-Nielsen holding and therefore, limited the application of Kinecta and Nelsen. (Talarides Decl., Ex. E, Reply arbitration brief, pp. 9-10.) Further, Garden Fresh Restaurant Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 678, cited in Petitioners' reply brief, is distinguishable. There, the parties presented the issue of whether class arbitration was available to the Court via a petition to compel arbitration before the issue was submitted to the arbitrator. Here, Petitioners request that the Court determine whether class arbitration is available after the parties voluntarily submitted the issue to the arbitrator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: REGENCY CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC VS. ELAJOU GROUP, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 14, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANTs ELAJOU GROUP, LLC, JUMA ELAJOU's MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Sanctions granted in the amount of $762 against Mark Hill and counsel David Nemecek Jr. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EXPERT COMMUNICATIONS, INC VS. GAIL TANAKA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Osc Re Contempt For Violation Of Preliminary Injunction And Tro Issued 4/18/14
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 14, 2015, Line 12, Osc Re Contempt For Violation Of Preliminary Injunction And Tro Issued 4/18/14. Grant. The court finds that defendant is in contempt of the temporary restraining order issued on April 21, 2014. Defendant copied plaintiff's files onto Sony flash drive No. 2 despite the clear order restraining such conduct. The court fines defendant $500 that is payable to the court by February 2, 2015. Plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorney's fees and costs for commencing this proceeding. Plaintiff shall provide a declaration at the hearing regarding the hours expended and counsel's hourly rate. Plaintiff shall provide a copy of that declaration to the court and also serve it on opposing counsel prior to commencement of the hearing. The court denies plaintiff's request to examine all of defendant's computers and storage media. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The proposed order shall have a blank line for the fees and costs. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ERIK WS NEWTON VS. TERRANCE C HEATH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Terrance C. Heath Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of This Court'S Tro Dated November 13, 2014
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 14, 2015, Line 14, Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Terrance C. Heath Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of This Court's Tro Dated November 13, 2014. Grant. The court finds that defendant is in contempt of the temporary restraining order issued on November 13, 2014. Defendant used partnership assets for purposes other than winding up the law firm and failed to provide access to partnership records. The court orders defendant immediately to provide access to plaintiff to the partnership's business records. The court fines defendant $900 that is payable to the court by February 2, 2015. Plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorney's fees and costs for commencing this proceeding. Plaintiff shall provide a declaration at the hearing regarding the hours expended and counsel's hourly rate. Plaintiff shall provide a copy of that declaration to the court and also serve it on opposing counsel prior to commencement of the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The proposed order shall have a blank line for the fees and costs. =(302/AJR)
